what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking it to a target image location and logging when it was added.
observations: Fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
target uses an image:// URI-like string: 'image://design-assets/icons/image (19)'
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
File body is empty after the frontmatter
standouts: Target string contains spaces and parentheses: 'image (19)'
Very minimal metadata with no title, caption, or uploader
concerns: Target string is not URL-encoded and contains spaces/parentheses, which may cause resolution issues
No descriptive label or filename separate from the target
No indication of who added the attachment or its file size/format
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that registers an image asset in the design-assets channel with a target pointer and timestamp.
questions: Does 'image (19)' refer to a filename, a display name, or an indexed asset?
What system resolves the image://design-assets/icons/... target?
Is there a corresponding actual image file elsewhere in the repository?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as an attachment metadata record matches the content: it records an image attachment, its channel, target, and addition time.
intention_signals: To track when an image attachment was added to a channel and to record a pointer to where that image can be found.
novelty: The target uses an image:// scheme with a channel path and a display-name-like segment containing spaces and parentheses; otherwise the record is a conventional metadata stub.
contribution: Provides a precise, timestamped attachment reference that a fused system can use to locate an image asset within the design-assets channel.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-6620f31d for an image in the design-assets channel, pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (19) and timestamped 2026-06-22T12:26:25Z.
intention_for_embedding: To log the addition of a design-assets image attachment and record its target location and creation time.
